title: leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. cantabricum
intro: Leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. cantabricum, also known as the Cantabric Oxeye Daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Cantabrian Mountains in northern Spain. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. cantabricum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
